issue.body:
### Goal
Make `.squarewright.yml`'s `budget.maxToolCalls`/`maxTokens` actually take effect — today they're parsed, seeded by `init`, and `WorkerRequest.budget` exists, but `runReview` never passes them, so the config field lies. Surfaced by both councils (a self-honesty gap on the tool's own surface).

### Context / background
`assemblyConfigSchema.budget` (`src/assembly/config.ts`) is validated; `src/init/default-config.ts` seeds `budget:{maxToolCalls:30}`; `WorkerRequest.budget?` (`src/pi/session.ts`) is ready — but `runReview` (`src/assembly/review.ts`) constructs `worker.run({context, lane, persona, systemPrompt})` with no `budget`.

### Acceptance criteria (ATDD)
- `runReview` threads `config.budget` into each `worker.run(...)` call. Test (stub worker) asserts the worker receives the config's budget.
- Absent `budget` → `worker.run` gets no budget (unchanged). Test.
- (If cheap) the worker actually honors `maxToolCalls` as a stop — else document that enforcement is the worker's responsibility and this only wires it through.

### Evidence / checks
`bun run verify:pr` green + the wiring test.

### Non-goals
No new budget fields; no mid-run token-abort (separate hard problem). Not the review-command spend-guard (#36).

### Stop condition
Surface if honoring `maxToolCalls` requires Pi-API capabilities that aren't exposed (then this only wires it through + documents the gap).
issue.title: Enforce config.budget (parsed but silently ignored)

pr.body:
Closes #40. Both councils flagged `config.budget` as a 'field that lies' — parsed + seeded by `init`, `WorkerRequest.budget` ready, but `runReview` never passed it.

## What changed
`runReview` now threads `config.budget` into every `worker.run(...)`.

## Honest scope (not overclaiming)
This makes budget **flow** to the worker so a future enforcer can honor it — but it's **advisory today**. A hard mid-run tool-call/token cap needs a Pi abort primitive we don't have (the same mid-run-abort gap the spend-guard documents; cc-dcp had no portable mechanism). Commented in the code as such; no pretend hard cap.

## Commands / CI
`bun run verify:pr` green (79 tests, +1: worker receives config.budget).

## What I need from the maintainer
No decision beyond merge/block. Hard enforcement is a separate spike (build a mid-run abort on Pi's primitives, if exposed).

pr.title: feat(assembly): wire config.budget to the worker (#40)
